# RetiringRoom

> Independent guide to IRCTC retiring rooms at Indian Railway stations. Compare tariffs, room types, AC and non-AC variants, and hourly booking availability across 120+ stations and growing.

## What this site covers

- Station-by-station retiring room information for major Indian Railway stations
- Tariff bands per room type (single AC/non-AC, double AC/non-AC, dormitory AC/non-AC)
- Hourly booking availability (3-hour minimum at select stations)
- Booking process, eligibility rules, and cancellation policies
- City-level and state-level rollups

## Booking is NOT done on this site

All bookings are made on the official IRCTC retiring room portal: https://www.rr.irctc.co.in

RetiringRoom.com is an independent informational site, not affiliated with Indian Railways or IRCTC.

## Site sections

- /stations — All indexed stations with tariff bands
- /cities — Browse retiring rooms by city
- /states — Browse retiring rooms by state
- /guides — Booking process, rules, room types, women-traveller and senior-citizen advice

## Key facts

- Confirmed or RAC PNR required to book any retiring room
- Standard slots: 12, 24, or 48 hours
- Hourly slots: 3-hour minimum at select stations
- Refund slabs: 90% (>48hr), 80% (24-48hr), 50% (4-24hr), nil (<4hr)
- One room per PNR at source, one at destination
- ID at check-in must match the name on the PNR
